cosmosdb.command.account.create.descript: NoSQL database for your web apps
cosmosdb.command.ai.deploy_instruction_f: Copy LLM instructions to user profile folder
cosmosdb.command.ai.remove_instruction_f: Remove LLM instructions from user profile folder
cosmosdb.configuration.batch.size: The batch size to be used when querying Azure Database resources.
cosmosdb.configuration.confirmation.styl: The behavior to use when confirming operations that cannot be undone, such as deleting resources.
Prompts with a warning dialog where you click a button to confirm deletion.
Prompts with a challenge where you select the correct number among multiple options to confirm deletion.
Prompts with an input box where you enter the name of the resource to confirm deletion.
cosmosdb.configuration.document.label.fi: The field values to display as labels in the treeview for Cosmos DB and MongoDB documents, in priority order
cosmosdb.configuration.emulator.port: Port to use when connecting to a CosmosDB Emulator instance
cosmosdb.configuration.endpoint.discover: Flag to enable/disable automatic redirecting of requests based on read/write operations.
cosmosdb.configuration.oauth.enable: Always authenticate using Entra ID RBAC when connecting to Cosmos DB NoSQL Accounts. [Enable RBAC and assign Roles](https://aka.ms/cosmos-native-rbac).
cosmosdb.configuration.operation.summari: Show detailed operation summaries, displaying messages for actions such as database drops, document additions, deletions, or similar events.
cosmosdb.configuration.save.prompt: Show warning dialog when uploading a document to the cloud.
cosmosdb.configuration.csv.separator: The separator to use when exporting data to a CSV file (i.e. `;`, | or `,`).
cosmosdb.configuration.cosmosDB.shell.pa: Full path to the folder and executable for starting the Azure Cosmos DB shell. Required for certain shell commands. By default, the system path is searched for 'cosmosdbshell'
cosmosdb.configuration.cosmosDB.shell.mc: Enable the Azure Cosmos DB shell Model Context Protocol Server.
The port to use for the Azure Cosmos DB Shell Model Context Protocol Server.
cosmosdb.configuration.preferredAuthenti: Select a preferred authentication method for Cosmos DB accounts.
Selects the best authentication method based on the account configuration
[LocalAuth](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/nosql/security/how-to-disable-key-based-authentication) must be enabled, this is not recommended for production environments
Sign in to VS Code using Entra ID
Only supported when running in Azure (i.e. a VM), [learn more](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cosmos-db/how-to-setup-managed-identity)
cosmosdb.configuration.authentication.ma: The Client ID of the Managed Identity to use when authenticating with Entra ID using a User-assigned Managed Identity.
cosmosdb.configuration.manage-llm-assets: Manage LLM assets. Allow the extension to keep LLM-related files updated.
cosmosdb.configuration.queryEditor.gener: When enabled, schema generation also considers query results to infer the container schema.
cosmosdb.configuration.accountOverview.h: Account Overview: peak normalized RU consumption (%) at or above which a container is flagged as Critical.
Account Overview: peak normalized RU consumption (%) at or above which a container is flagged as Needs Attention.
cosmosdb.configuration.accountOverview.p: Account Overview: number of physical partitions to list in the Partition Key Distribution Health ranked list.
cosmosdb.configuration.accountOverview.a: Account Overview: per-physical-partition p99 normalized RU consumption (%) at or above which a partition counts as saturated. Drives both the Hot Partition Risk and Sustained Throttling / Under-provisioning advisories derived from your telemetry.

Account Overview: per-physical-partition p99 normalized RU consumption (%) below which a partition still has headroom. A saturated busiest partition alongside a cooler one is a hot partition (re-key); every partition saturated is uniform under-provisioning (add RU/s).

Account Overview: container request rate throttled (HTTP 429, sum(429)/sum(total) as a %) at or above which throttling counts as active — escalating a Hot Partition Risk to high severity and gating the Sustained Throttling / Under-provisioning advisory derived from your telemetry.

Account Overview: 99th-percentile normalized RU consumption (%) below which, sustained over 7 days, an Over-provisioning advisory is derived from your telemetry. Banding on p99 rather than the peak ignores brief spikes.
Account Overview: peak normalized RU consumption (%) a workload must reach for an Autoscale Candidate advisory — below this, right-size the manual throughput instead of enabling autoscale.
Account Overview: average normalized RU consumption (%) a workload must stay at or below for an Autoscale Candidate advisory (mostly idle between bursts).

Account Overview: peak-to-average normalized RU ratio at or above which, on manual throughput, an Autoscale Candidate advisory is derived from your telemetry (a genuine burst rather than steady load).

Account Overview: projected days-to-limit horizon at or below which a Storage Growth Risk advisory is derived from your telemetry. A physical partition whose least-squares storage trajectory reaches the 50 GiB split ceiling within this many days is flagged, with severity graded by how soon.

Account Overview: balance ratio (coolest physical-partition size divided by busiest) below which, when the busiest partition is also material (at least 1 GiB), a Storage Skew Risk advisory is derived from your telemetry. 1.0 is perfectly balanced; severity grades by the busiest partition's proximity to the 50 GiB split ceiling.
Account Overview: index-to-data storage ratio above which, when no index paths are excluded, an Indexing Cost Risk advisory is derived from your telemetry.
cosmosdb.configuration.quickStart.enable: When enabled, Quick Start tips are shown in the Query Editor after a major or minor extension update. Disable this to turn off Quick Start tips entirely, including the replay button.
cosmosdb.configuration.accountOverview.a: Account Overview: peak request units consumed in any single bucket, at or below which a container counts as idle and an Idle Container advisory is derived from your telemetry. A container that never spends more than this near-zero floor in any bucket over the 30-day window served no real traffic — its whole reservation (manual) or idle floor (autoscale) is recoverable.

Account Overview: average autoscale utilization (percent of the configured max) at or above which, combined with a low peak-to-average ratio, an Autoscale To Manual advisory is derived from your telemetry — a workload sustained near its max barely uses autoscale's elasticity and is cheaper on manual throughput.

Account Overview: peak-to-average ratio of autoscale utilization at or below which the load counts as steady for the Autoscale To Manual advisory. A flat, high series (little spikiness) is the autoscale-to-manual signal.

Account Overview: account-total average-to-peak request-unit ratio below which a Serverless Candidate advisory is derived from your telemetry — a low, sporadic workload with long idle stretches whose shape suits serverless pay-per-request billing.

Account Overview: account-total peak request units per second above which a Serverless Candidate advisory considers the workload real enough to evaluate. Below this the account is idle, which is the Idle Container decommission case rather than a serverless migration.

Account Overview: account-total peak request units per second at or below which a Serverless Candidate advisory still fits serverless. Above this single-partition ceiling the workload is steered away, since serverless caps at 5,000 request units per second per physical partition.

Account Overview: minimum number of query executions in the analysis window below which there is too little query traffic to judge cross-partition fan-out (Cross-partition Query advisory, from Log Analytics query telemetry).

Account Overview: average number of physical partitions a query shape must touch per run to count as a cross-partition (fanning-out) query. A shape that inherently targets a single logical partition touches one; higher values indicate the query does not filter on the partition key.

Account Overview: share (percent) of query executions that fan out across partitions at or above which the Cross-partition Query advisory is rated High severity (otherwise Medium down to the medium floor).
Account Overview: share (percent) of query executions that fan out across partitions below which the Cross-partition Query advisory does not fire.

Account Overview: share (percent) of query executions fanning out across partitions at or above which the partition key itself is considered structurally misaligned with the workload, raising a Partition Key Misalignment advisory (re-key the container) that supersedes the per-query Cross-partition Query advisory.
Account Overview: cross-partition share (percent) at or above which the Partition Key Misalignment advisory is rated High severity (otherwise Medium).

Account Overview: share (percent) of request units consumed by write operations at or above which a container's workload counts as write-dominant, a precondition for the Uncontrolled Ingestion advisory (from Log Analytics data-plane telemetry).
Account Overview: rate-limited (429) request rate (percent) at or above which a write-dominant container counts as throttling, firing the Uncontrolled Ingestion advisory.
Account Overview: rate-limited (429) request rate (percent) at or above which the Uncontrolled Ingestion advisory is rated High severity (otherwise Medium).
Account Overview: minimum number of data-plane requests in the analysis window below which there is too little traffic to judge the Uncontrolled Ingestion advisory.

Account Overview: minimum number of requests below which a collection is treated as inactive for the window and excluded from the Shared-throughput Starvation advisory (from Log Analytics data-plane telemetry).

Account Overview: database-wide rate-limited (429) request rate (percent) at or above which a shared-throughput database pool counts as under pressure, a precondition for the Shared-throughput Starvation advisory.
Account Overview: share (percent) of the shared pool's request units one collection must consume to count as the monopolizer in the Shared-throughput Starvation advisory.
Account Overview: rate-limited (429) request rate (percent) at or above which a low-consumption sibling collection counts as a starved victim in the Shared-throughput Starvation advisory.

Account Overview: share (percent) of the shared pool's request units at or below which a throttled sibling collection counts as a starved victim (consuming little while being throttled) in the Shared-throughput Starvation advisory.
cosmosdb.configuration.experimental.migr: (Experimental) Show token usage estimates in the Migration Assistant UI.
(Experimental) Enable the Cosmos DB Migration (Preview) feature. When disabled, the Cosmos DB Migrations node and its commands are hidden.
